key_insight: In disordered perovskite nanocrystal solids, exciton transport is governed more by energetic disorder than by structural polydispersity.
engineering_relevance: Ligand-chain engineering can be used to tune nanocrystal disorder and optimize light-emitting, laser, and solar-device performance.
method: Resolved exciton dynamics in space, time, and energy.
Compared nanocrystal solids synthesized with different alkylamine ligand lengths.
Linked transport behavior to structural versus energetic disorder.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that longer ligands produced more monodisperse but smaller, more quantum-confined nanocrystals, yet exciton transport was less efficient than in more polydisperse samples.
watch_item: Improved size uniformity alone may not improve transport; increased quantum confinement can raise energetic disorder and reduce exciton mobility.
paper_title: Exciton Transport in Disordered Perovskite Nanocrystal Solids
